items.requirement: Retrieve implementation details from ~/pyquda-agent and ~/PyQUDA, especially runnable examples, tests, IO helpers, source helpers, inversion paths, contraction patterns, and output conventions.
items.notes: The audit aggregates pinned PyQUDA references from the representative plan plus the validated workflow-plan artifacts, covering examples, tests, io/__init__.py, source.py, core.py, gamma.py, and family-specific references such as the PCAC example.

items.requirement: When local repository knowledge is insufficient for physics conventions, consult authoritative sources and record chosen conventions with citations.
items.notes: Citations are recorded in the implementation plan; they are currently refreshed from a curated manifest rather than chosen by live task-time browsing.

items.requirement: Parse the user request into a structured task specification instead of directly writing code.
items.notes: The CLI emits a structured task artifact before generation.

items.requirement: Detect underspecified fields and ask follow-up questions rather than inventing missing parameters.
items.notes: Missing required fields stop complete generation and surface follow-up questions.

items.requirement: Distinguish physics choices, PyQUDA implementation choices, and cluster/runtime choices.
items.notes: The implementation plan has dedicated sections for all three categories.

items.requirement: Generate a complete Python script only after required fields are resolved.
items.notes: The command returns needs_input or unsupported before generation when fields are unresolved.

items.requirement: Refuse to label output as complete if placeholders, TODOs, fake helper calls, or guessed APIs remain.
items.notes: Validation rejects forbidden tokens and asserts required real API strings.

items.requirement: Validate generated scripts against real PyQUDA interfaces and example usage patterns.
items.notes: Static structure and reference grounding are validated; direct execution evidence is tracked through the unified run-path probe artifact. Full numerical validation still depends on a working PyQUDA runtime.

items.requirement: Generated script uses real PyQUDA imports, source/inversion/contraction APIs, and real gauge IO paths.
items.notes: The script uses real local gauge input and reference-grounded API choices; the direct probe shows runtime preflight is reached.

items.requirement: Generated script is derived from and traceable to concrete upstream references in ~/PyQUDA.
items.notes: The plan and generated script trace the workflow back to concrete upstream PyQUDA references.

items.requirement: Generated complete script contains no TODO/pass/placeholder sections.
items.notes: The generated script is guarded by token-level validation and regression tests.

items.requirement: The system explains which task fields were user-specified, clarified interactively, and which conventions were chosen from references.
items.notes: Field provenance is tracked in field_sources/field_resolution, and convention decisions plus clarification traces are carried in the implementation plan.

items.requirement: The system supports both --backend api and --backend codex.
items.notes: Both backends drive the same structured generation path. Parity and non-mocked execution artifacts track either real use or explicit fallback. Current backend availability states: {'api': 'usable', 'auto': 'usable', 'codex': 'usable'}.

items.requirement: The system reliably selects among the currently supported workflow families from rough or direct runnable requests, with the pion_2pt family retaining both grounded gauge-entry and propagator-entry paths.
items.notes: The current supported set spans the grounded pion_2pt, pion_pcac, pion_dispersion, meson_spec, proton_2pt, rho_vector, quark_propagator, ape_smear, hyp_smear, stout_smear, and wilson_flow families, including the validated propagator-entry branches where applicable. Unsupported requests must remain explicit.

items.requirement: Generated script is complete and HPC-ready: real APIs, explicit cluster/runtime assumptions, no placeholders, and suitable for handoff to a properly configured PyQUDA cluster environment.
items.notes: This repository now treats grounded HPC handoff readiness as the default done condition. Local runtime proof remains optional evidence and must be reported separately.

items.requirement: Optional: have local evidence that the current machine can numerically execute the generated script.
items.notes: Local runtime proof is informative only. Current probe status='runtime_missing', runtime_level='environment_missing'. The generated script may still be complete for HPC handoff even when this workstation lacks CuPy, pyquda, or built QUDA bindings.

items.requirement: The main run path distinguishes generation success from execution/probe success and persists sibling probe artifacts for auditable runtime evidence.
items.notes: The top-level run result carries execution_status while runtime_evidence.generated_script_probe links the sibling .probe.json artifact and its command path.

items.requirement: Validation artifacts preserve product-facing lifecycle evidence, including product_status plus generation/execution phases for both backend execution checks and supported-workflow integration checks.
items.notes: The compact validation artifacts now record rough-request and direct-run product-facing phases so product clients can audit the same lifecycle contract outside individual CLI invocations.

items.requirement: Product-behavior regression artifacts cover clarification routing, backend degradation, terminal execution awareness, terminal repair guidance, supported workflow generation, explicit unsupported refusal, and probe artifact consistency.
items.notes: The current v9 product-behavior groups are ['rough_request_clarification', 'backend_degradation', 'terminal_execution_awareness', 'supported_workflow_generation', 'hpc_handoff_quality', 'runtime_recovery_guidance', 'explicit_unsupported_refusal', 'probe_artifact_consistency'].

items.requirement: Rough but reasonable supported requests enter clarification instead of collapsing into pseudocode or premature unsupported output.
items.notes: The supported-workflow validator checks rough request shapes across the current workflow catalog, while the v9 product-behavior validator checks the product-facing clarification path explicitly.

items.requirement: Explicit supported requests generate grounded scripts together with coherent generation/probe/runtime status reporting.
items.notes: Direct supported runs are expected to reach generated/probe-available, generated/runtime-blocked, or runtime-proved states with matching lifecycle cards and sibling artifacts.

items.requirement: Backend failures, network failures, runtime-environment blockers, and probe-harness failures expose clear next actions instead of silent degradation.
items.notes: This requirement is proved by the current backend degradation, terminal execution-awareness, probe consistency, and explicit refusal groups in the v9 product-behavior validator.

items.requirement: Explicit unsupported requests expose the nearest grounded recovery path with either a copyable retry action or an explicit physics-choice gate, without letting backend-fix guidance override the main unsupported action.
items.notes: The v9 product-behavior summary now records the unsupported-action contract directly: {'backend_fix_should_not_be_primary': True, 'choice_required_kind': 'choose_supported_variant', 'copyable_retry_kind': 'retry_supported_workflow'}.

items.requirement: A realistic natural-language task suite covers ambiguous requests, explicit supported requests, and near-boundary unsupported variants with explicit nearest-grounded recovery expectations.
items.notes: The current v11 task suite covers 16 realistic requests across ['ambiguous_meson', 'explicit_supported', 'flow', 'non_grounded_meson_like', 'propagator', 'smear', 'unsupported_baryon', 'unsupported_flow_variant', 'unsupported_propagator_variant', 'unsupported_smear_variant'], including unsupported propagator/smear/flow boundary variants.

items.requirement: Execution-readiness artifacts distinguish backend repairability, runtime dependency blockers, probe-harness failures, and runtime-side handoff blockers with explicit next actions.
items.notes: The current v12 execution-readiness surface covers 8 cases across ['backend truly usable', 'backend degraded but continueable', 'backend repair path', 'runtime blocked but handoff ready', 'runtime blocked due to missing dependencies', 'runtime blocked due to probe harness failure', 'runtime blocked due to input visibility / output writability / cluster assumptions', 'real tasks less reliant on manual fallback'].

items.requirement: V13 validation artifacts distinguish codex usable vs fallback-only state and local runtime-proved vs exact remaining blockers without blurring those boundaries.
items.notes: The current v13 codex/runtime-readiness surface covers 4 cases across ['codex backend usable or explicitly fallback_only', 'runtime proved or exact remaining blockers', 'backend repair contract', 'runtime repair contract', 'docs and audit consistency'].
